evidence: The structural IR is a foundational subsystem within the top-level PRISM monorepo architecture.
Language adapters are one of the major repo subsystems declared in the workspace crate split.
Store and history crates form a durable architectural slice of the repo.
prism-core indexing and refresh orchestration is a central architecture subsystem.
Memory is a first-class subsystem in the repo architecture and product model.
Coordination and plan runtime are major architectural responsibilities with dedicated crates and specs.
Projection and query are central repo subsystems surfaced across PRISM tools and docs.
Validation is treated as a cross-cutting architectural concern rather than a local testing detail.
Concept publication is a dedicated architectural layer spanning core events, projections, query, and MCP.
Inference and curation form a durable architectural slice rather than a one-off workflow.
The MCP runtime is the main agent-facing product surface layered on top of the core crates.
Compact tools are a distinct architectural slice of the repo’s agent-facing surface.
The dashboard is an explicit first-class surface inside the overall PRISM architecture.
The CLI is a thin but real operator-facing architectural surface in the repo.
All language adapter crates depend on prism-ir through prism-parser to emit the shared node and edge schema.
Store and history layers persist and replay IR identities, anchors, and event metadata rather than defining their own schema.
Index refresh orchestrates parsing and resolution into the shared IR model before any higher-level projections exist.
The workspace indexer relies on language adapters to turn concrete files into parse results.
Index refresh materializes and updates its state through the store and history layers.
Memory recall and reanchoring rely on persisted history, snapshots, and stored memory projections.
Plan, task, claim, and artifact runtime state is keyed by IR identities and shared event metadata.
Queries and projections read graph and history snapshots from the persistence layer.
Projection and query surfaces incorporate recall, outcomes, and memory-backed context.
The Prism read surface exposes plan, task, blocker, claim, and artifact views from coordination state.
Concept publication becomes useful only after projection and query layers materialize concept packets and retrieval views.
Curator output is only durable once it flows through the concept and knowledge publication path.
The MCP runtime serves query, discovery, and concept-resolution results produced by the projection and query layer.
MCP tools and resources expose plan, task, claim, artifact, and blocker state through the coordination runtime.
Concept mutation, concept resolution, and relation mutation are served from the MCP runtime surface.
Compact tools are the staged specialization of the broader MCP runtime surface for agent-first workflows.
Compact tools are thin orchestration over the query and projection layer rather than an independent semantic engine.
The dashboard is a specialized product surface inside prism-mcp built on the general MCP runtime.
Dashboard views surface active operations, coordination state, and mutation timelines from the runtime.
The CLI delegates indexing and workspace session behavior into prism-core rather than implementing its own runtime.
The validation plan explicitly starts with structural truth and identity correctness.
The validation pipeline includes memory anchoring and PRISM memory re-anchoring validation.
Coordination correctness is one of the named validation layers in docs/VALIDATION.md.
Projection validation and MCP/query-surface validation are explicit parts of the repo validation model.
Concept maintenance and validation policy both require evidence-backed promotion and relation maintenance.
The validation pipeline explicitly calls out PRISM MCP and query-surface validation.
Compact tools are part of the query-surface path that the repo expects to validate and dogfood directly.
The dashboard spec treats trust and validation as visible first-class concerns rather than hidden internals.
This concept is the concrete module cluster behind the higher-level workspace indexing and refresh subsystem.
Workspace session hydration and refresh runtime are a second internal slice of the broader indexing/refresh subsystem.
Publication guards are the policy and validation sub-layer inside the concept/publication pipeline.
Query/resource serving is a concrete internal slice of the broader MCP runtime surface.
Mutation hosting and session-state handling are core internal responsibilities of the MCP runtime surface.
Daemon lifecycle and runtime health are another internal slice of the MCP runtime surface.
Recall/scoring is a concrete internal slice of the broader memory system.
Outcome/session history is a distinct internal slice of the broader memory system.
Semantic and structural retrieval models are a distinct internal slice of the broader memory system.
The state/type/runtime model is the foundational internal slice of the coordination subsystem.
Queries, mutations, blockers, and helpers are the executable behavior slice of the coordination subsystem.
The indexing pipeline relies on language adapters to convert files into parse results before resolution and reanchoring.
Indexing, resolution, and reanchoring all target the shared IR identity and graph model.
Workspace session refresh explicitly coordinates memory snapshot loading and refresh behavior.
Hydrated workspace sessions and patch outcomes are loaded from and synchronized back to persisted workspace state.
Publication guards encode evidence, trust, provenance, and quality thresholds that align directly with the repo validation philosophy.
This serving layer is just the transport/runtime wrapper around the underlying projection and query semantics.
Mutation hosting persists concepts, relations, memories, and outcomes through the publication/mutation pipeline.
The same mutation host also fronts coordination and claim/artifact updates through the MCP surface.
Mutation contract correctness and session-state handling are central to the repo’s dogfooding and validation loop.
The runtime lifecycle is useful only because it hosts and refreshes live WorkspaceSession-backed state.
Recall/scoring is distinct from semantic/structural models, but they are commonly used together to improve retrieval quality.
Outcome and replay history are part of the evidence loops the repo expects to validate and dogfood directly.
Semantic and structural retrieval models need explicit validation because their outputs can look plausible while drifting semantically.
The executable behavior layer in prism-coordination is implemented over the shared state/types/runtime model.
Operational plan/task policy is one of the repo areas where validation and dogfooding are explicitly required.
Coordination state and overlays are keyed by the same stable identities and event model exposed by prism-ir.
Derived projection indexes are the projection-side internal slice of the broader read layer.
Symbol and change-context bundling is one internal slice of the overall projection/query layer.
Coordination-aware query views are another internal slice of the overall projection/query layer.
The JS/TS ABI is the language-level contract layer attached to the broader projection/query surface.
Projection indexes hydrate published concepts and concept relations emitted by the publication pipeline.
These query views are built over coordination and plan runtime state rather than over raw graph structure alone.
Symbol/change read bundles rely on the derived indexes and rankings produced by the projection layer.
These are exactly the kinds of context bundles that can look convincing while drifting, so they need direct dogfooding and validation.
Task/plan query views are part of the surfaced behavior the repo expects to validate end to end.
The JS query ABI and the MCP runtime are separate layers, but in practice they travel together as the agent-facing programmable interface.
Rust and Python parser/syntax/path modules are the executable-code subfamily inside the broader adapter stack.
The code adapters emit shared PRISM nodes, edges, anchors, and references through the common IR contract.
Markdown, JSON, TOML, and YAML adapters are the document/config half of the shared adapter family.
Document and config adapters still materialize shared IR identities and structure even when their inputs are not executable source.
Curator and curator_support are the concrete execution slice inside the broader inference-and-curation subsystem.
Curator proposals only become durable repo knowledge after they flow into the concept and publication path.
Curator output quality is checked through the same dogfooding and validation loop that guards durable knowledge promotion.
Published plan files, layout helpers, and artifact path helpers are the filesystem publication sub-layer of the broader publication pipeline.
Layout and durable-plan writes rely on the publication rules that decide what may safely become repo knowledge.
Published plan artifacts are a durability surface for coordination state even though the layout helpers are not the coordination engine itself.
Path/layout publication is only trustworthy when validation catches misplaced or stale durable artifacts.
Graph, identity, primitives, and parse are the static-schema sublayer inside the broader structural IR concept.
Anchor, change, event, and lineage modules are the temporal-traceability sublayer of prism-ir.
Plan and coordination schema live in prism-ir as a specialized shared type family rather than a separate subsystem.
The runtime coordination layer is built on top of the shared plan/coordination IR schema defined in prism-ir.
SQLite, graph, and persist-batch modules are the primary durable-store sublayer inside persistence and history.
The store persists graph snapshots and ids defined by prism-ir rather than inventing a separate semantic schema.
Memory projection and memory_store are the memory-specific persistence slice inside the broader persistence/history layer.
This persistence slice exists to hydrate and durably back the repo’s memory subsystem rather than the raw graph alone.
Resolver, snapshot, and history store form the temporal replay half of the persistence/history subsystem.
Lineage snapshots and resolution replay IR identities, events, and anchors defined in prism-ir.
Locate/open/workset/text-fragment handling is the navigation half of the compact tool surface.
Compact discovery and bounded opening are orchestration over locate/search/query primitives rather than an independent semantic engine.
Expand, concept, and task-brief form the deeper decoding half of the compact tool surface.
Compact expand and task decoding are derived read surfaces over query/projection data, not raw storage owners.
Compact concept views are only useful because the publication pipeline materializes durable concept packets and relations.
Task brief decoding makes this compact view cluster a frequent companion to coordination and plan runtime reasoning.
CLI argument definition, parsing, and command dispatch are the input half of the CLI surface.
Many CLI commands are thin wrappers around workspace indexing/session behavior provided by prism-core.
Runtime execution, MCP lifecycle control, and terminal display are the execution half of the CLI surface.
The CLI’s MCP commands control daemon startup, restart, status, and related runtime lifecycle behavior.
Router/assets/types define the browser-facing transport boundary inside the dashboard surface.
Dashboard routing and asset delivery are exposed from the same prism-mcp runtime that hosts the product surface.
Events and read models are the live-state half of the dashboard surface.
Dashboard read models surface active tasks, blockers, and runtime coordination state rather than only static metadata.
The validation principles, pipeline, PRISM plan, and trust gates are the policy half of the validation subsystem.
Feedback capture, projection checks, metrics dashboards, and correction loops are the live calibration half of validation and dogfooding.
Validation checks and scorecards are materialized through projection state rather than only handwritten notes.
The validation feedback loop relies on the hosted mutation path that records `validation_feedback` entries into repo state.
The validation spec explicitly calls for a metrics dashboard, making this loop a frequent companion to the dashboard surface.
The inferred-edge store and mutation path are the non-curator half of the repo’s inference-and-curation subsystem.
Live inferred-edge capture and promotion rely on the MCP mutation/session host that accepts `infer_edge` and `curator_promote_edge` actions.
Inferred edges are explicitly called out in the validation plan as a bounded overlay that must not silently rewrite authoritative structure.
Concept, relation, and intent packet materialization is one half of the semantic projection subsystem.
These projections are built from concept and relation publication events rather than raw source structure alone.
Co-change and validation indexes are the derived-index half of the semantic projection subsystem.
These projection indexes are derived from history snapshots and persisted outcome/projection state.
Symbol and source resolution is the direct-read half of the symbol/change query cluster.
Symbol and relation reads rely on projected graph/context structures even when they also touch raw source slices.
Impact and outcome reads are the judgment-oriented half of the symbol/change query cluster.
Outcome history and validation context are backed by the repo’s memory and outcome subsystems.
Runtime coordination and plan-overlay access is one half of the coordination query cluster.
These query views are a direct read surface over the live coordination and plan runtime state.
Completion and insight heuristics are the higher-judgment half of the coordination query cluster.
Plan completion and insight evaluation only make sense over current coordination and plan runtime state.
Runtime prelude and generated reference docs are one half of the JS-facing ABI surface.
Shared client/server payload contracts are the schema half of the JS-facing ABI surface.
The JS contract surface must stay aligned with the server’s query/resource payload shapes.
Live query execution and semantic context serving are one half of the MCP query/resource surface.
This serving path is a thin runtime wrapper over the repo’s projection and query layer.
Host resources and schema-backed resource publication are the other half of the MCP query/resource surface.
Many host resources expose published concepts, relations, memories, and related durable knowledge state.
Argument decoding and schema publication are one half of the MCP mutation/session host.
Schema examples and argument validation are explicitly part of the repo’s MCP/query-surface validation story.
Session state, hosted mutations, and runtime views are the execution half of the MCP mutation/session host.
Many hosted mutations and follow-up runtime views touch shared task, claim, artifact, and plan state.
Daemon/process/proxy control is one half of the runtime lifecycle surface.
Server capability, runtime state, and diagnostics are the health/status half of the runtime lifecycle surface.
Server diagnostics, runtime status, and health reporting are only trustworthy if the repo validates them directly.
Concept and relation event streams are the append-only event half of the publication pipeline.
These event streams are most useful once projection indexes materialize them into queryable packets.
Published knowledge artifacts and memory event logs are the durable-log half of the publication pipeline.
Memory event logs are consumed by the repo’s memory subsystem rather than living as an isolated publication detail.
Durable knowledge and memory publication logs still rely on the publication/trust rules that decide what is safe to persist.
Scoring signals and text helpers are one half of the memory recall/scoring subsystem.
Composite routing and entry storage are the orchestration/storage half of memory recall and scoring.
Outcome replay is one half of the memory outcomes/session-history branch.
Outcome replay matters because it preserves prior failures and validations, so it needs direct dogfooding and validation.
Session/episodic storage is the continuity-storage half of the outcomes/session-history branch.
Session memory composes structural and semantic modules into the session-scoped memory runtime.
Structural feature extraction and structural recall are one half of the model-backed memory branch.
Structural recall still uses the shared recall/base-signal and text-overlap helpers when it ranks entries.
Semantic backend configuration/runtime is the embedding-aware half of the model-backed memory branch.
Semantic recall still layers its backend signals onto the shared recall/text scoring substrate.
Indexer orchestration and snapshot restoration are one half of the core indexing pipeline.
This orchestration path loads prior graph, history, outcomes, coordination, and projection snapshots from persisted state.
Parsing, resolution, and reanchor logic are the structural-truth half of the core indexing pipeline.
This flow only exists because language adapters produce unresolved parse results and structure for later resolution.
Parse, resolution, and reanchor all target the shared node, edge, and identity model in prism-ir.
Session refresh state and filesystem watch handling are one half of the workspace session runtime.
Memory reanchor and patch outcome recording are the persistence-feedback half of the workspace session runtime.
This path explicitly reanchors persisted session memory and records outcome events into the memory subsystem.
Reanchored memory and patch-derived validation deltas need direct validation because they can look right while silently drifting.
state.rs and types.rs provide the stored coordination state and domain schema that make up the coordination state model.
compat.rs and runtime.rs export plan graph compatibility and runtime overlays as the second half of the coordination state model.
queries.rs and blockers.rs implement the read-side and blocker policy half of coordination operations.
Coordination queries and blocker evaluation execute over the shared coordination state store and types.
mutations.rs and helpers.rs implement the write-side and shared policy helper half of coordination operations.
Coordination mutations and policy helpers operate against the shared coordination state model and runtime overlays.
Mutation-side coordination policy needs direct validation because it can violate plan and claim invariants without schema changes.
prism-parser provides the shared adapter contract and fingerprint utilities beneath both code and document/config adapter families.
Rust and Python adapters implement the shared prism-parser LanguageAdapter contract and fingerprint helpers.
Markdown, JSON, TOML, and YAML adapters reuse the shared prism-parser document and intent/fingerprint helpers.
The Rust parser/syntax/path split is one concrete branch of the code language adapter family.
The Rust adapter implements LanguageAdapter and reuses shared parse input/result and fingerprint helpers from prism-parser.
The Python parser/syntax/path split is one concrete branch of the code language adapter family.
The Python adapter implements LanguageAdapter and reuses shared parse input/result and fingerprint helpers from prism-parser.
The Markdown adapter is one concrete branch of the document/config adapter family.
The Markdown adapter uses shared document-name, document-path, fingerprint, and intent-target helpers from prism-parser.
JSON, TOML, and YAML config walkers are the structured-value branch of the document/config adapter family.
The JSON, TOML, and YAML adapters all reuse shared document, fingerprint, whole-file-span, and intent helper logic from prism-parser.
Backend config, context bounding, schema rendering, and Codex process launch are the execution half of the curator flow.
Curator backend prompting and sandboxed execution need direct dogfooding because they can produce plausible but weak proposals.
Proposal typing and rule synthesis are the proposal-construction half of the curator flow.
Curator synthesis explicitly consumes memories and outcomes when generating structural/semantic memory, risk, and validation proposals.
Curator context and proposal rules consume co-change and validation-check projections when ranking or deduplicating proposals.
Locate ranking and text-fragment candidate generation are the first-hop navigation half of compact discovery/opening.
Compact locate reranks semantic search results and text-derived symbol candidates produced from the underlying query/projection layer.
Open and workset follow-through are the bounded read-assembly half of compact discovery/opening.
Compact open/workset resolves handles, previews slices, and assembles supporting reads from the underlying query surface.
Concept packet resolution and bounded handle expansion are one half of compact expansion/concept views.
Compact concept/expand decode runtime is a presentation layer over concept packets, validations, lineage, and other query results.
Task-brief summaries are the coordination-specific summary half of compact expansion/concept views.
Task-brief summaries are built directly from coordination task, blocker, claim, conflict, and plan-runtime query state.
Argument decoding and mutation schemas are one half of the authenticated mutation host surface, including authenticated coordination and heartbeat contracts.
Session context, hosted mutation execution, and follow-up runtime views are the execution half of the authenticated mutation host.
Authenticated mutation contracts, provenance stamping, and hosted runtime behavior are central to the repo’s MCP dogfooding loop.
The authenticated mutation host fronts coordination, claim, artifact, and heartbeat mutations through `prism_mutate` over the shared coordination runtime.
Before any authoritative write is persisted, the authenticated mutation host stamps event actors and execution-context snapshots through the principal/provenance layer.
Lease ownership, resume/reclaim policy, and heartbeat enforcement derive holder identity from authoritative principal actors recorded on mutation events.
Principal actors and execution-context snapshots are durable IR event types rather than MCP-only transport metadata.
Live inferred-edge capture and curator promotion rely on the authenticated mutation host that accepts `infer_edge` and curator-driven mutation actions.
